# nxt-pick-datetime documentation

> Date time picker for Angular

> Generated by compodoc 2.0.0 at 2026-07-10T17:27:12.344Z, llm-md export.

## Components

### CalendarComponent

File: `src/lib/date-time/calendar/calendar.component.ts`
Selector: `nxt-date-time-calendar`

Inputs:
- `pickerMoment: T | undefined = undefined` — The current picker moment
- `dateTimeFilter: DateFilter<T>` — Date filter for the month and year view
- `firstDayOfWeek: number = 0` — Set the first day of week
- `hideOtherMonths: boolean = false` — Whether to hide dates in other months at the start or end of the current month
- `max: T | undefined = undefined` — The maximum selectable date
- `min: T | undefined = undefined` — The minimum selectable date
- `selected: T | undefined = undefined` — The currently selected moment
- `selecteds: Array<T | undefined> = []`
- `selectMode: SelectMode`
- `startView: "month" | "year" | "multi-years" = 'month'` — The view that the calendar should start in

Outputs:
- `monthSelected: T` — Emits the selected month This doesn't imply a change on the selected date.
- `pickerMomentChange: T` — Emits when the currently picker moment changes
- `selectedChange: T | undefined` — Emits when the currently selected date changes
- `userSelection: void` — Emits when any date is selected
- `yearSelected: T` — Emits the selected year This doesn't imply a change on the selected date.

Properties:
- `currentView: unknown = linkedSignal({ source: () => this.startView(), computation: v => v })`
- `dateTimeFilterForViews: unknown = () => {...}` — Date filter for the month and year view
- `isInRangeMode: unknown = computed(() => { const selectMode = this.selectMode() return ( selectMode === 'range' || selectMode === 'rangeFrom' || selectMode === 'rangeTo' ) })`
- `isInSingleMode: unknown = computed(() => this.selectMode() === 'single')`
- `isMonthView: unknown = computed(() => this.currentView() === 'month')`
- `nextButtonLabel: unknown = computed(() => { const currentView = this.currentView() if (currentView === 'month') { return this.pickerIntl.nextMonthLabel() } else if (currentView === 'year'…`
- `periodButtonLabel: unknown = computed(() => this.isMonthView() ? this.pickerIntl.switchToMultiYearViewLabel() : this.pickerIntl.switchToMonthViewLabel())`
- `periodButtonText: unknown = computed(() => this.isMonthView() ? this.dateTimeAdapter.format( this.pickerMoment(), this.dateTimeFormats.monthYearLabel ) : this.dateTimeAdapter.getYearName(t…`
- `pickerMoment: unknown = computed(() => { const value = this.dateTimeAdapter.deserialize(this._pickerMoment()) return this.getValidDate(value) || this.dateTimeAdapter.now() })`
- `prevButtonLabel: unknown = computed(() => { const currentView = this.currentView() if (currentView === 'month') { return this.pickerIntl.prevMonthLabel() } else if (currentView === 'year'…`
- `showControlArrows: unknown = computed(() => this.currentView() !== 'multi-years')`

Methods:
- `dateSelected(date?: T): void`
- `focusActiveCell(): void` — Focus to the host element
- `goToDateInView(date: T, view: "month" | "year" | "multi-years"): void` — Change the pickerMoment value and switch to a specific view
- `handlePickerMomentChange(date: T): void` — Change the pickerMoment value
- `nextButtonEnabled(): boolean` — Whether the next period button is enabled.
- `nextClicked(): void` — Handles user clicks on the next button.
- `prevButtonEnabled(): boolean` — Whether the previous period button is enabled.
- `previousClicked(): void` — Handles user clicks on the previous button.
- `selectMonthInYearView(normalizedMonth: T): void`
- `selectYearInMultiYearView(normalizedYear: T): void`
- `toggleViews(): void` — Toggle between month view and year view
- `userSelected(): void`

### DateTimeComponent

File: `src/lib/date-time/date-time-picker/date-time-picker.component.ts`
Selector: `nxt-date-time`

Inputs:
- `disabled: boolean | undefined = undefined` — Whether the date time picker should be disabled
- `isOpen: boolean = false` — Whether the calendar is open
- `startAt: T | undefined = undefined` — The date to show when picker opens Defaults to selected date (if available) or current date.
- `backdropClass: string | string[]` — Custom class for the picker backdrop
- `panelClass: string | string[]` — Custom class for the picker overlay pane
- `pickerMode: PickerMode = 'popup'` — The style the picker would open as 'popup' - display picker attached to the trigger element 'dialog' - display picker as a dialog
- `pickerType: PickerType = 'both'` — Set the type of the dateTime picker 'both' - show both calendar and timer 'calendar' - show only calendar 'timer' - show only timer
- `scrollStrategy: ScrollStrategy` — The scroll strategy when the picker is open (see CDK documentation )
- `firstDayOfWeek: number = 0` — Set the first day of week Valid values are from 0 \[Sunday\] to 6 \[Saturday\]
- `hideOtherMonths: boolean = false` — If true, don't show dates from preious / next month on the calendar month view
- `hour12Timer: boolean = false` — When true, the timer will be in 12-hour format mode
- `showSecondsTimer: boolean = false` — When true, shows a timer to configure the seconds value
- `startView: ViewType = 'month'` — The view that the calendar should start on
- `stepHour: number = 1` — Hours to change per step
- `stepMinute: number = 1` — Minutes to change per step
- `stepSecond: number = 1` — Seconds to change per step

Outputs:
- `close: void` — Callback when the picker is closed
- `open: void` — Callback when the picker is opened
- `confirmSelectedChange: Array<T | undefined> | T` — Emit when the selected value has been confirmed
- `disabledChange: boolean` — Emits when the date time picker is disabled
- `isOpenChange: boolean` — Emits when picker open state changes
- `monthSelected: T` — Emits selected month in year view This doesn't imply a change on the selected date.
- `yearSelected: T` — Emits selected year in multi-year view This doesn't imply a change on the selected date.

Properties:
- `dateTimeFilter: unknown = computed(() => this.dtInput()?.dateTimeFilter())`
- `disabled: unknown = linkedSignal({ source: () => this._disabled(), computation: value => value ?? !!this.dtInput()?.disabled() })`
- `dtInput: unknown = this._dtInput.asReadonly()`
- `isInRangeMode: unknown = computed(() => !!this.dtInput()?.isInRangeMode())`
- `isInSingleMode: unknown = computed(() => !!this.dtInput()?.isInSingleMode())`
- `isOpen: unknown = signal(false)`
- `max: unknown = computed(() => this.dtInput()?.max())` — The maximum selectable date
- `min: unknown = computed(() => this.dtInput()?.min())` — The minimum selectable date
- `selected: unknown = signal<T | undefined>(undefined)`
- `selecteds: unknown = signal(new Array<T | undefined>())`
- `selectMode: unknown = computed(() => this.dtInput()?.selectMode())`
- `startAt: unknown = computed(() => { // If an explicit startAt is set we start there, otherwise we start at whatever the currently // selected value is. const startAt = this._start…`
- `dateTimeAdapter: unknown = inject<DateTimeAdapter<T>>(DateTimeAdapter)`
- `dateTimeChecker: unknown = signal((dateTime: T | undefined) => ( !!dateTime && (!this.dateTimeFilter() || this.dateTimeFilter()!(dateTime)) && (!this.min() || this.dateTimeAdapter.compare…` — Date Time Checker to check if the give dateTime is selectable
- `dateTimeFormats: unknown = inject<DateTimeFormats>(NXT_DATE_TIME_FORMATS)`
- `formatString: unknown = computed(() => this.pickerType() === 'both' ? this.dateTimeFormats.fullPickerInput : this.pickerType() === 'calendar' ? this.dateTimeFormats.datePickerInput : t…`
- `` id: unknown = `nxt-dt-picker-${nextUniqueId++}` ``
- `monthSelected: OutputEmitterRef<T>`
- `pickerMode: Signal<PickerMode>`
- `pickerType: Signal<PickerType>`
- `yearSelected: OutputEmitterRef<T>`

Methods:
- `close(): void` — Hide the picker
- `confirmSelect(_event?: any): void` — Confirm the selected value
- `ngOnDestroy(): void`
- `open(event?: PointerEvent): void`
- `registerInput(inputDirective: DateTimeInputDirective<T>): void`
- `select(date: T[] | T): void` — Selects the given date
- `selectMonth(normalizedMonth: T): void` — Emits selected month in year view
- `selectYear(normalizedYear: T): void` — Emits the selected year in multi-year view
- `getValidDate(obj: any): T | undefined`

### DateTimeInlineComponent

File: `src/lib/date-time/date-time-inline/date-time-inline.component.ts`
Selector: `nxt-date-time-inline`

Inputs:
- `disabled: boolean = false` — Whether the date time picker should be disabled
- `startAt: T | undefined = undefined` — The date to show when picker opens Defaults to selected date (if available) or current date.
- `value: T | undefined = undefined`
- `values: Array<T | undefined> | undefined = undefined`
- `dateTimeFilter: DateFilter<T>` — A function to filter available date & time
- `max: T | undefined = undefined` — The maximum valid date
- `min: T | undefined = undefined` — The minimum valid date
- `pickerType: PickerType = 'both'` — Set the type of the dateTime picker 'both' - show both calendar and timer 'calendar' - show only calendar 'timer' - show only timer
- `selectMode: SelectMode = 'single'` — The picker select mode 'single' - select a single date 'range' - select a range \[from, to\] 'rangeFrom' - an open range with a fixed start date 'rangeTo' - an open range with a fixed end date
- `firstDayOfWeek: number = 0` — Set the first day of week Valid values are from 0 \[Sunday\] to 6 \[Saturday\]
- `hideOtherMonths: boolean = false` — If true, don't show dates from preious / next month on the calendar month view
- `hour12Timer: boolean = false` — When true, the timer will be in 12-hour format mode
- `showSecondsTimer: boolean = false` — When true, shows a timer to configure the seconds value
- `startView: ViewType = 'month'` — The view that the calendar should start on
- `stepHour: number = 1` — Hours to change per step
- `stepMinute: number = 1` — Minutes to change per step
- `stepSecond: number = 1` — Seconds to change per step

Outputs:
- `monthSelected: T` — Emits selected month in year view This doesn't imply a change on the selected date.
- `yearSelected: T` — Emits selected year in multi-year view This doesn't imply a change on the selected date.

Properties:
- `disabled: unknown = linkedSignal({ source: () => this._disabled(), computation: v => v })`
- `isInRangeMode: unknown = computed(() => ( this.selectMode() === 'range' || this.selectMode() === 'rangeFrom' || this.selectMode() === 'rangeTo' ))`
- `isInSingleMode: unknown = computed(() => this.selectMode() === 'single')`
- `isOpen: unknown = signal(true).asReadonly()`
- `pickerMode: unknown = signal<PickerMode>('inline').asReadonly()`
- `selected: unknown = linkedSignal({ source: () => this.value(), computation: v => v })`
- `selecteds: unknown = linkedSignal({ source: () => this.values(), computation: v => v || [] })`
- `startAt: unknown = computed(() => { const startAt = this._startAt() if (startAt) { return startAt } const selectMode = this.selectMode() if (selectMode === 'single') { return this…`
- `value: unknown = computed(() => { let value = this._value() value = this.dateTimeAdapter.deserialize(value) value = this.getValidDate(value) return value })`
- `values: unknown = computed(() => { let values = this._values() if (values && values.length > 0) { values = values.map(v => { v = this.dateTimeAdapter.deserialize(v) v = this.getV…`
- `dateTimeAdapter: unknown = inject<DateTimeAdapter<T>>(DateTimeAdapter)`
- `dateTimeChecker: unknown = signal((dateTime: T | undefined) => ( !!dateTime && (!this.dateTimeFilter() || this.dateTimeFilter()!(dateTime)) && (!this.min() || this.dateTimeAdapter.compare…` — Date Time Checker to check if the give dateTime is selectable
- `dateTimeFilter: Signal<DateFilter | undefined>`
- `dateTimeFormats: unknown = inject<DateTimeFormats>(NXT_DATE_TIME_FORMATS)`
- `formatString: unknown = computed(() => this.pickerType() === 'both' ? this.dateTimeFormats.fullPickerInput : this.pickerType() === 'calendar' ? this.dateTimeFormats.datePickerInput : t…`
- `` id: unknown = `nxt-dt-picker-${nextUniqueId++}` ``
- `max: Signal<T | undefined>`
- `min: Signal<T | undefined>`
- `monthSelected: OutputEmitterRef<T>`
- `pickerType: Signal<PickerType>`
- `selectMode: Signal<SelectMode | undefined>`
- `yearSelected: OutputEmitterRef<T>`

Methods:
- `ngOnInit(): void`
- `registerOnChange(fn: any): void`
- `registerOnTouched(fn: any): void`
- `select(date: T[] | T): void`
- `selectMonth(normalizedMonth: T): void` — Emits selected month in year view
- `selectYear(normalizedYear: T): void` — Emits the selected year in multi-year view
- `setDisabledState(isDisabled: boolean): void`
- `writeValue(value: any): void`
- `getValidDate(obj: any): T | undefined`

### MonthViewComponent

File: `src/lib/date-time/calendar-month-view/calendar-month-view.component.ts`
Selector: `nxt-date-time-month-view`

Inputs:
- `dateTimeFilter: DateFilter<T>` — A function used to filter which dates are selectable
- `firstDayOfWeek: number = this._firstDayOfWeek` — Define the first day of a week Sunday: 0 ~ Saturday: 6
- `hideOtherMonths: boolean = false` — Whether to hide dates in other months at the start or end of the current month
- `max: T | undefined = undefined` — The maximum selectable date
- `min: T | undefined = undefined` — The minimum selectable date
- `pickerMoment: T | undefined = undefined`
- `selected: T | undefined = undefined` — The currently selected date
- `selecteds: Array<T | undefined> = []`
- `selectMode: SelectMode = 'single'` — The select mode of the picker;

Outputs:
- `pickerMomentChange: T` — Emits when any date is activated
- `selectedChange: T | undefined` — Callback to invoke when a new date is selected
- `userSelection: void` — Callback to invoke when any date is selected.

Properties:
- `_daysData: unknown = computed(() => { if (!this.pickerMoment()) { return } let todayDate: number | undefined // the first weekday of the month const startWeekdayOfMonth = this.dateT…`
- `activeCell: unknown = computed(() => { if (this.pickerMoment()) { return ( this.dateTimeAdapter.getDate(this.pickerMoment()) + this.firstRowOffset() - 1 ) } return })`
- `days: unknown = computed(() => { const days = this._daysData()?.days if (days != undefined) return this._days = days return this._days })`
- `firstRowOffset: unknown = computed(() => { const firstRowOffset = this._daysData()?.firstRowOffset if (firstRowOffset != undefined) return this._firstRowOffset = firstRowOffset return th…`
- `isInRangeMode: unknown = computed(() => ( this.selectMode() === 'range' || this.selectMode() === 'rangeFrom' || this.selectMode() === 'rangeTo' ))`
- `isInSingleMode: unknown = computed(() => this.selectMode() === 'single')`
- `selectedDates: unknown = computed(() => { /** * Set the selectedDates value. * In single mode, it has only one value which represent the selected date * In range mode, it would has two …` — An array to hold all selectedDates' value the value is the day number in current month
- `todayDate: unknown = computed(() => { const todayDate = this._daysData()?.todayDate if (todayDate != undefined) return this._todayDate = todayDate return this._todayDate })` — The date of the month that today falls on.
- `weekdays: unknown = computed(() => { const longWeekdays = this.dateTimeAdapter.getDayOfWeekNames('long') const shortWeekdays = this.dateTimeAdapter.getDayOfWeekNames('short') const…`

Methods:
- `handleCalendarKeydown(event: KeyboardEvent): void` — Handle keydown event on calendar body
- `isSameMonth(dateLeft?: T, dateRight?: T): boolean` — Check if the give dates are defined and in the same month
- `selectCalendarCell(cell: CalendarCell): void` — Handle a calendarCell selected

### MultiYearViewComponent

File: `src/lib/date-time/calendar-multi-year-view/calendar-multi-year-view.component.ts`
Selector: `nxt-date-time-multi-year-view`

Inputs:
- `pickerMoment: T | undefined = undefined` — Settable signal for pickerMoment
- `dateTimeFilter: DateFilter<T>` — A function used to filter which dates are selectable
- `max: T | undefined = undefined` — The maximum selectable date
- `min: T | undefined = undefined` — The minimum selectable date
- `selected: T | undefined = undefined` — The currently selected date
- `selecteds: Array<T | undefined> = []`
- `selectMode: SelectMode = 'single'` — The select mode of the picker;

Outputs:
- `change: T` — Callback to invoke when a new month is selected
- `keyboardEnter: void` — Emits when use keyboard enter to select a calendar cell
- `pickerMomentChange: T` — Emits when any date is activated
- `yearSelected: T` — Emits the selected year This doesn't imply a change on the selected date.

Properties:
- `activeCell: unknown = computed(() => { if (this.pickerMoment()) { return this.dateTimeAdapter.getYear(this.pickerMoment()) % (YEARS_PER_ROW * YEAR_ROWS) } return })`
- `isInRangeMode: unknown = computed(() => ( this.selectMode() === 'range' || this.selectMode() === 'rangeFrom' || this.selectMode() === 'rangeTo' ))`
- `isInSingleMode: unknown = computed(() => this.selectMode() === 'single')`
- `nextButtonLabel: unknown = this.pickerIntl.nextMultiYearLabel.asReadonly()`
- `pickerMoment: unknown = computed(() => { const value = this.dateTimeAdapter.deserialize(this._pickerMoment()) return this.getValidDate(value) || this.dateTimeAdapter.now() })`
- `prevButtonLabel: unknown = this.pickerIntl.prevMultiYearLabel.asReadonly()`
- `selectedYears: unknown = computed(() => { let selectedYears = new Array<number | undefined>() if (this.isInSingleMode() && this.selected()) { selectedYears[0] = this.dateTimeAdapter.get…`
- ``tableHeader: unknown = computed(() => { const years = this.years() if (years && years.length > 0) { return `${years[0][0].displayValue} ~ ${years[YEAR_ROWS - 1][YEARS_PER_ROW - 1].dis…``
- `todayYear: unknown = computed(() => this.yearsData().todayYear)`
- `years: unknown = computed(() => this.yearsData().years)`

Methods:
- `handleCalendarKeydown(event: KeyboardEvent): void`
- `nextEnabled(): boolean` — Whether the next period button is enabled
- `nextYearList(event: any): void` — Generate the next year list
- `previousEnabled(): boolean` — Whether the previous period button is enabled
- `prevYearList(event: any): void` — Generate the previous year list
- `selectCalendarCell(cell: CalendarCell): void` — Handle a calendarCell selected

### TimerComponent

File: `src/lib/date-time/timer/timer.component.ts`
Selector: `nxt-date-time-timer`

Inputs:
- `hour12Timer: boolean = false` — Whether the timer is in hour12 format
- `max: T | undefined = undefined` — The maximum selectable date time
- `min: T | undefined = undefined` — The minimum selectable date time
- `pickerMoment: T | undefined = undefined` — The current picker moment
- `showSecondsTimer: boolean = false` — Whether to show the second's timer
- `stepHour: number = 1` — Hours to change per step
- `stepMinute: number = 1` — Minutes to change per step
- `stepSecond: number = 1` — Seconds to change per step

Outputs:
- `selectedChange: T`

Properties:
- `downHourButtonLabel: unknown = this.pickerIntl.downHourLabel.asReadonly()`
- `downHourEnabled: unknown = computed(() => ( !this.min() || this.compareHours(-this.stepHour(), this.min()!) > -1 ))` — Check if the down hour button is enabled
- `downMinuteButtonLabel: unknown = this.pickerIntl.downMinuteLabel.asReadonly()`
- `downMinuteEnabled: unknown = computed(() => ( !this.min() || this.compareMinutes(-this.stepMinute(), this.min()!) > -1 ))` — Check if the down minute button is enabled
- `downSecondButtonLabel: unknown = this.pickerIntl.downSecondLabel.asReadonly()`
- `downSecondEnabled: unknown = computed(() => ( !this.min() || this.compareSeconds(-this.stepSecond(), this.min()!) > -1 ))` — Check if the down second button is enabled
- `hour12ButtonLabel: unknown = computed(() => this.isPM() ? this.pickerIntl.hour12PMLabel() : this.pickerIntl.hour12AMLabel())`
- `hourBoxValue: unknown = computed(() => this.hoursWithPM().hours)` — The value would be displayed in hourBox. We need this because the value displayed in hourBox it not the same as the hourValue when the timer is in hour12Timer mode.
- `hourValue: unknown = computed(() => this.dateTimeAdapter.getHours(this.pickerMoment()))`
- `minuteValue: unknown = computed(() => this.dateTimeAdapter.getMinutes(this.pickerMoment()))`
- `secondValue: unknown = computed(() => this.dateTimeAdapter.getSeconds(this.pickerMoment()))`
- `upHourButtonLabel: unknown = this.pickerIntl.upHourLabel.asReadonly()`
- `upHourEnabled: unknown = computed(() => ( !this.max() || this.compareHours(this.stepHour(), this.max()!) < 1 ))` — Check if the up hour button is enabled
- `upMinuteButtonLabel: unknown = this.pickerIntl.upMinuteLabel.asReadonly()`
- `upMinuteEnabled: unknown = computed(() => ( !this.max() || this.compareMinutes(this.stepMinute(), this.max()!) < 1 ))` — Check if the up minute button is enabled
- `upSecondButtonLabel: unknown = this.pickerIntl.upSecondLabel.asReadonly()`
- `upSecondEnabled: unknown = computed(() => ( !this.max() || this.compareSeconds(this.stepSecond(), this.max()!) < 1 ))` — Check if the up second button is enabled

Methods:
- `focus(): void` — Focus to the host element
- `setHourValue(hours: number): void`
- `setHourValueViaInput(hours: number): void` — Set the hour value via typing into timer box input We need this to handle the hour value when the timer is in hour12 mode
- `setMeridiem(event: any): void`
- `setMinuteValue(minutes: number): void`
- `setSecondValue(seconds: number): void`

### YearViewComponent

File: `src/lib/date-time/calendar-year-view/calendar-year-view.component.ts`
Selector: `nxt-date-time-year-view`

Inputs:
- `dateTimeFilter: DateFilter<T>` — A function used to filter which dates are selectable
- `max: T | undefined = undefined` — The maximum selectable date
- `min: T | undefined = undefined` — The minimum selectable date
- `pickerMoment: T | undefined = undefined`
- `selected: T | undefined = undefined` — The currently selected date
- `selecteds: Array<T | undefined> = []`
- `selectMode: SelectMode = 'single'` — The select mode of the picker;

Outputs:
- `change: T` — Callback to invoke when a new month is selected
- `keyboardEnter: void` — Emits when use keyboard enter to select a calendar cell
- `monthSelected: T` — Emits the selected year This doesn't imply a change on the selected date.
- `pickerMomentChange: T` — Emits when any date is activated

Properties:
- `activeCell: unknown = computed(() => { if (this.pickerMoment()) { return this.dateTimeAdapter.getMonth(this.pickerMoment()) } return })`
- `isInRangeMode: unknown = computed(() => ( this.selectMode() === 'range' || this.selectMode() === 'rangeFrom' || this.selectMode() === 'rangeTo' ))`
- `isInSingleMode: unknown = computed(() => this.selectMode() === 'single')`
- `months: unknown = computed(() => { const months = this.monthsData()?.months if (months != undefined) return this._months = months return this._months })`
- `selectedMonths: unknown = computed(() => { /** * Set the selectedMonths value * In single mode, it has only one value which represent the month the selected date in * In range mode, it w…` — An array to hold all selectedDates' month value the value is the month number in current year
- `todayMonth: unknown = computed(() => { const todayMonth = this.monthsData()?.todayMonth if (todayMonth != undefined) return this._todayMonth = todayMonth return this._todayMonth })`

Methods:
- `handleCalendarKeydown(event: KeyboardEvent): void` — Handle keydown event on calendar body
- `selectCalendarCell(cell: CalendarCell): void` — Handle a calendarCell selected

## Directives

### DateTimeDirective

File: `src/lib/class/date-time.class.ts`

Inputs:
- `firstDayOfWeek: number = 0` — Set the first day of week Valid values are from 0 \[Sunday\] to 6 \[Saturday\]
- `hideOtherMonths: boolean = false` — If true, don't show dates from preious / next month on the calendar month view
- `hour12Timer: boolean = false` — When true, the timer will be in 12-hour format mode
- `showSecondsTimer: boolean = false` — When true, shows a timer to configure the seconds value
- `startView: ViewType = 'month'` — The view that the calendar should start on
- `stepHour: number = 1` — Hours to change per step
- `stepMinute: number = 1` — Minutes to change per step
- `stepSecond: number = 1` — Seconds to change per step

Properties:
- `dateTimeAdapter: unknown = inject<DateTimeAdapter<T>>(DateTimeAdapter)`
- `dateTimeChecker: unknown = signal((dateTime: T | undefined) => ( !!dateTime && (!this.dateTimeFilter() || this.dateTimeFilter()!(dateTime)) && (!this.min() || this.dateTimeAdapter.compare…` — Date Time Checker to check if the give dateTime is selectable
- `dateTimeFilter: Signal<DateFilter | undefined>`
- `dateTimeFormats: unknown = inject<DateTimeFormats>(NXT_DATE_TIME_FORMATS)`
- `disabled: Signal<boolean>`
- `formatString: unknown = computed(() => this.pickerType() === 'both' ? this.dateTimeFormats.fullPickerInput : this.pickerType() === 'calendar' ? this.dateTimeFormats.datePickerInput : t…`
- `` id: unknown = `nxt-dt-picker-${nextUniqueId++}` ``
- `isInRangeMode: Signal<boolean>`
- `isInSingleMode: Signal<boolean>`
- `isOpen: Signal<boolean>`
- `max: Signal<T | undefined>`
- `min: Signal<T | undefined>`
- `monthSelected: OutputEmitterRef<T>`
- `pickerMode: Signal<PickerMode>`
- `pickerType: Signal<PickerType>`
- `selected: Signal<T | undefined>`
- `selecteds: Signal<Array<T | undefined>>`
- `selectMode: Signal<SelectMode | undefined>`
- `startAt: Signal<T | undefined>`
- `yearSelected: OutputEmitterRef<T>`

Methods:
- `getValidDate(obj: any): T | undefined`
- `select(date: T | Array<T | undefined>): void`
- `selectMonth(normalizedMonth: T): void`
- `selectYear(normalizedYear: T): void`

### DateTimeInputDirective

File: `src/lib/date-time/date-time-picker-input.directive.ts`
Selector: `input[nxtDateTime]`

Inputs:
- `disabled: boolean = false` — Whether the date & time picker input is disabled
- `value: T = undefined`
- `values: Array<T | undefined> = new Array<T | undefined>()`
- `nxtDateTime: DateTimeComponent<T> = undefined` — The date & time picker this input is associated with
- `dateTimeFilter: DateFilter<T>` — A function to filter available date & time
- `max: T | undefined = undefined` — The maximum valid date
- `min: T | undefined = undefined` — The minimum valid date
- `rangeSeparator: string = '~'` — The character to separate the 'from' and 'to' in input value
- `selectMode: SelectMode = 'single'` — The picker select mode 'single' - select a single date 'range' - select a range \[from, to\] 'rangeFrom' - an open range with a fixed start date 'rangeTo' - an open range with a fixed end date

Outputs:
- `dateTimeChange: DateTimeInputEvent<T>` — Callback to invoke when change event is fired on this \<input\>
- `dateTimeInput: DateTimeInputEvent<T>` — Callback to invoke when an input event is fired on this \<input\> .
- `disabledChange: boolean` — Emits when the disabled state has changed
- `valueChange: Array<T | undefined> | T | undefined` — Emits when the value changes (either due to user input or programmatic change)

Properties:
- `disabled: unknown = linkedSignal({ source: () => this._disabled(), computation: v => v })`
- `dtPicker: unknown = signal<DateTimeComponent<T> | undefined>(undefined)` — The date-time-picker that this input is associated with
- `isInRangeMode: unknown = computed(() => { const selectMode = this.selectMode() return ( selectMode === 'range' || selectMode === 'rangeFrom' || selectMode === 'rangeTo' ) })`
- `isInSingleMode: unknown = computed(() => this.selectMode() === 'single')`
- `value: unknown = this.parsedValue.asReadonly()`
- `values: unknown = this.parsedValues.asReadonly()`

Methods:
- `handleBlurOnHost(_event: Event): void`
- `handleChangeOnHost(_event: any): void`
- `handleInputOnHost(event: any): void`
- `handleKeydownOnHost(event: KeyboardEvent): void` — Open the picker when user hold alt + ArrowDown
- `ngAfterContentInit(): void`
- `ngOnDestroy(): void`
- `ngOnInit(): void`
- `registerOnChange(fn: any): void`
- `registerOnTouched(fn: any): void`
- `registerOnValidatorChange(fn: function): void`
- `setDisabledState(isDisabled: boolean): void`
- `validate(c: AbstractControl): any`
- `writeValue(value: any): void`

### DateTimeTriggerDirective

File: `src/lib/date-time/date-time-picker-trigger.directive.ts`
Selector: `[nxtDateTimeTrigger]`

Inputs:
- `disabled: boolean = undefined`
- `dialogOrigin: boolean = false` — Use the event to calculate origin when opening in dialog mode
- `nxtDateTimeTrigger: DateTimeComponent<T> = undefined`

Properties:
- `disabled: unknown = computed(() => this._disabled() ?? !!this.dtPicker()?.disabled())`

Methods:
- `handleClickOnHost(event: PointerEvent): void`

## Services / Injectables

### DateTimeIntl

File: `src/lib/date-time/date-time-picker-intl.service.ts`
providedIn: `root`

Properties:
- ``cancelBtnLabel: unknown = signal($localize`:@@nxt-pick-datetime.cancel:Cancel`)`` — A label for the cancel button
- ``downHourLabel: unknown = signal($localize`:@@nxt-pick-datetime.down-hour:Minus a hour`)`` — A label for the down hour button (used by screen readers).
- ``downMinuteLabel: unknown = signal($localize`:@@nxt-pick-datetime.down-minute:Minus a minute`)`` — A label for the down minute button (used by screen readers).
- ``downSecondLabel: unknown = signal($localize`:@@nxt-pick-datetime.down-second:Minus a second`)`` — A label for the down second button (used by screen readers).
- ``hour12AMLabel: unknown = signal($localize`:@@nxt-pick-datetime.time-am:AM`)`` — A label for the hour12 button (AM)
- ``hour12PMLabel: unknown = signal($localize`:@@nxt-pick-datetime.time-pm:PM`)`` — A label for the hour12 button (PM)
- ``nextMonthLabel: unknown = signal($localize`:@@nxt-pick-datetime.next-month:Next month`)`` — A label for the next month button (used by screen readers)
- ``nextMultiYearLabel: unknown = signal($localize`:@@nxt-pick-datetime.next-multi-year:Next 21 years`)`` — A label for the next multi-year button (used by screen readers)
- ``nextYearLabel: unknown = signal($localize`:@@nxt-pick-datetime.next-year:Next year`)`` — A label for the next year button (used by screen readers)
- ``prevMonthLabel: unknown = signal($localize`:@@nxt-pick-datetime.prev-month:Previous month`)`` — A label for the previous month button (used by screen readers)
- ``prevMultiYearLabel: unknown = signal($localize`:@@nxt-pick-datetime.prev-multi-year:Previous 21 years`)`` — A label for the previous multi-year button (used by screen readers)
- ``prevYearLabel: unknown = signal($localize`:@@nxt-pick-datetime.prev-year:Previous year`)`` — A label for the previous year button (used by screen readers)
- ``rangeFromLabel: unknown = signal($localize`:@@nxt-pick-datetime.from:From`)`` — A label for the range 'from' in picker info
- ``rangeToLabel: unknown = signal($localize`:@@nxt-pick-datetime.to:To`)`` — A label for the range 'to' in picker info
- ``setBtnLabel: unknown = signal($localize`:@@nxt-pick-datetime.set:Set`)`` — A label for the set button
- ``switchToMonthViewLabel: unknown = signal($localize`:@@nxt-pick-datetime.switch-to-month:Change to month view`)`` — A label for the 'switch to month view' button (used by screen readers)
- ``switchToMultiYearViewLabel: unknown = signal($localize`:@@nxt-pick-datetime.switch-to-year-month:Choose month and year`)`` — A label for the 'switch to year view' button (used by screen readers)
- ``upHourLabel: unknown = signal($localize`:@@nxt-pick-datetime.up-hour:Add a hour`)`` — A label for the up hour button (used by screen readers).
- ``upMinuteLabel: unknown = signal($localize`:@@nxt-pick-datetime.up-minute:Add a minute`)`` — A label for the up minute button (used by screen readers).
- ``upSecondLabel: unknown = signal($localize`:@@nxt-pick-datetime.up-second:Add a second`)`` — A label for the up second button (used by screen readers).

### MomentDateTimeAdapter

File: `moment-adapter/src/date-time-adapter.class.ts`

Properties:
- `locale: unknown = signal<any>(undefined)` — The locale to use for all dates
- `millisecondsInDay: number = 86400000` — total milliseconds in a day
- `milliseondsInMinute: number = 60000` — total milliseconds in a minute

Methods:
- `addCalendarDays(date: Moment, amount: number): Moment`
- `addCalendarMonths(date: Moment, amount: number): Moment`
- `addCalendarYears(date: Moment, amount: number): Moment`
- `clone(date: Moment): Moment`
- `createDate(year: number, month: number, date: number): Moment`
- `createDate(year: number, month: number, date: number, hours: number = 0, minutes: number = 0, seconds: number = 0): Moment`
- `deserialize(value: any): Moment | undefined` — Returns the given value if given a valid Moment or undefined. Deserializes valid ISO 8601 strings ( https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt ) and valid Date objects into valid Moments and empty string into undefined. Returns an invalid date for all other values.
- `differenceInCalendarDays(dateLeft: Moment, dateRight: Moment): number`
- `format(date: Moment, displayFormat: any): string`
- `getDate(date: Moment): number`
- `getDateNames(): string[]`
- `getDay(date: Moment): number`
- `getDayOfWeekNames(style: "long" | "short" | "narrow"): string[]`
- `getHours(date: Moment): number`
- `getMinutes(date: Moment): number`
- `getMonth(date: Moment): number`
- `getMonthNames(style: "long" | "short" | "narrow"): string[]`
- `getNumDaysInMonth(date: Moment): number`
- `getSeconds(date: Moment): number`
- `getTime(date: Moment): number`
- `getYear(date: Moment): number`
- `getYearName(date: Moment): string`
- `invalid(): Moment`
- `isDateInstance(obj: any): boolean`
- `isEqual(dateLeft: Moment, dateRight: Moment): boolean`
- `isSameDay(dateLeft: Moment, dateRight: Moment): boolean`
- `isValid(date: Moment): boolean`
- `now(): Moment`
- `parse(value: any, parseFormat: any): Moment | undefined`
- `setHours(date: Moment, amount: number): Moment`
- `setLocale(locale: string): void`
- `setMinutes(date: Moment, amount: number): Moment`
- `setSeconds(date: Moment, amount: number): Moment`
- `toIso8601(date: Moment): string`
- `clampDate(date: T, min?: T, max?: T): T` — Clamp the given date between min and max dates.
- `compare(first?: T, second?: T): number` — Compare two given dates 1 if the first date is after the second, -1 if the first date is before the second 0 if dates are equal.
- `compareYear(first?: T, second?: T): number` — Check if two given dates are in the same year 1 if the first date's year is after the second, -1 if the first date's year is before the second 0 if two given dates are in the same year

### NativeDateTimeAdapter

File: `native-adapter/src/date-time-adapter.class.ts`

Properties:
- `useUtcForDisplay: boolean` — Whether to use timeZone: 'utc' with Intl.DateTimeFormat when formatting dates. Without this Intl.DateTimeFormat sometimes chooses the wrong timeZone, which can throw off the result. (e.g. in the en-US locale new Date(1800, 7, 14).toLocaleDateString() will produce '8/13/1800' .
- `locale: unknown = signal<any>(undefined)` — The locale to use for all dates
- `millisecondsInDay: number = 86400000` — total milliseconds in a day
- `milliseondsInMinute: number = 60000` — total milliseconds in a minute

Methods:
- `addCalendarDays(date: Date, amount: number): Date`
- `addCalendarMonths(date: Date, amount: number): Date`
- `addCalendarYears(date: Date, amount: number): Date`
- `clone(date: Date): Date`
- `createDate(year: number, month: number, date: number, hours: number = 0, minutes: number = 0, seconds: number = 0): Date`
- `deserialize(value: any): Date | undefined` — Returns the given value if given a valid Date or undefined. Deserializes valid ISO 8601 strings ( https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt ) into valid Dates and empty string into undefined. Returns an invalid date for all other values.
- `differenceInCalendarDays(dateLeft: Date, dateRight: Date): number | undefined`
- `format(date: Date, displayFormat: any): string`
- `getDate(date: Date): number`
- `getDateNames(): string[]`
- `getDay(date: Date): number`
- `getDayOfWeekNames(style: "long" | "short" | "narrow"): string[]`
- `getHours(date: Date): number`
- `getMinutes(date: Date): number`
- `getMonth(date: Date): number`
- `getMonthNames(style: "long" | "short" | "narrow"): string[]`
- `getNumDaysInMonth(date: Date): number`
- `getSeconds(date: Date): number`
- `getTime(date: Date): number`
- `getYear(date: Date): number`
- `getYearName(date: Date): string`
- `invalid(): Date`
- `isDateInstance(obj: any): boolean`
- `isEqual(dateLeft: Date, dateRight: Date): boolean`
- `isSameDay(dateLeft: Date, dateRight: Date): boolean`
- `isValid(date: Date): boolean`
- `now(): Date`
- `parse(value: any, _parseFormat: any): Date | undefined`
- `setHours(date: Date, amount: number): Date`
- `setMinutes(date: Date, amount: number): Date`
- `setSeconds(date: Date, amount: number): Date`
- `toIso8601(date: Date): string`
- `clampDate(date: T, min?: T, max?: T): T` — Clamp the given date between min and max dates.
- `compare(first?: T, second?: T): number` — Compare two given dates 1 if the first date is after the second, -1 if the first date is before the second 0 if dates are equal.
- `compareYear(first?: T, second?: T): number` — Check if two given dates are in the same year 1 if the first date's year is after the second, -1 if the first date's year is before the second 0 if two given dates are in the same year
- `setLocale(locale: any): void` — Sets the locale used for all dates.

## Classes

### CalendarCell

File: `src/lib/date-time/calendar-body/calendar-body.component.ts`

Properties:
- `ariaLabel: string`
- `cellClass: string`
- `displayValue: string`
- `enabled: boolean`
- `out: boolean`
- `value: number`

### DateTimeAdapter

File: `src/lib/class/date-time-adapter.class.ts`

Properties:
- `locale: unknown = signal<any>(undefined)` — The locale to use for all dates
- `millisecondsInDay: number = 86400000` — total milliseconds in a day
- `milliseondsInMinute: number = 60000` — total milliseconds in a minute

Methods:
- `addCalendarDays(date: T | undefined, amount: number): T` — Add the specified number of days to the given date
- `addCalendarMonths(date: T | undefined, amount: number): T` — Add the specified number of months to the given date
- `addCalendarYears(date: T | undefined, amount: number): T` — Add the specified number of years to the given date
- `clampDate(date: T, min?: T, max?: T): T` — Clamp the given date between min and max dates.
- `clone(date?: T): T` — Clone the given date
- `compare(first?: T, second?: T): number` — Compare two given dates 1 if the first date is after the second, -1 if the first date is before the second 0 if dates are equal.
- `compareYear(first?: T, second?: T): number` — Check if two given dates are in the same year 1 if the first date's year is after the second, -1 if the first date's year is before the second 0 if two given dates are in the same year
- `createDate(year: number, month: number, date: number): T` — Creates a date with the given year, month, date, hour, minute and second. Does not allow over/under-flow of the month and date.
- `createDate(year: number, month: number, date: number, hours: number, minutes: number, seconds: number): T`
- `deserialize(value: any): T | undefined` — Attempts to deserialize a value to a valid date object. This is different from parsing in that deserialize should only accept non-ambiguous, locale-independent formats (e.g. a ISO 8601 string). The default implementation does not allow any deserialization, it simply checks that the given value is already a valid date object or null. The \<mat-datepicker\> will call this method on all of it's input properties that accept dates. It is therefore possible to support passing values from your backend directly to these properties by overriding this method to also deserialize the format used by your backend.
- `differenceInCalendarDays(dateLeft?: T, dateRight?: T): number | undefined` — Get the number of calendar days between the given dates. If dateLeft is before dateRight, it would return positive value If dateLeft is after dateRight, it would return negative value
- `format(date: T | undefined, displayFormat: any): string` — Formats a date as a string according to the given format.
- `getDate(date?: T): number` — Get the day num of the given date
- `getDateNames(): string[]` — Gets a list of names for the dates of the month.
- `getDay(date?: T): number` — Get the day of the week of the given date 0 -- Sunday 6 -- Saturday
- `getDayOfWeekNames(style: "long" | "short" | "narrow"): string[]` — Get a list of week names
- `getHours(date?: T): number` — Get the hours of the given date
- `getMinutes(date?: T): number` — Get the minutes of the given date
- `getMonth(date?: T): number` — Get the month of the given date 0 -- January 11 -- December
- `getMonthNames(style: "long" | "short" | "narrow"): string[]` — Get a list of month names
- `getNumDaysInMonth(date?: T): number` — Gets the number of days in the month of the given date.
- `getSeconds(date?: T): number` — Get the seconds of the given date
- `getTime(date?: T): number` — Get the milliseconds timestamp of the given date
- `getYear(date?: T): number` — Get the year of the given date
- `getYearName(date?: T): string` — Gets the name for the year of the given date.
- `invalid(): T` — Gets date instance that is not valid.
- `isDateInstance(obj: any): boolean` — Checks whether the given object is considered a date instance by this DateTimeAdapter.
- `isEqual(dateLeft?: T, dateRight?: T): boolean` — Check if the give dates are equal
- `isSameDay(dateLeft?: T, dateRight?: T): boolean` — Check if the give dates are the same day
- `isValid(date?: T): boolean` — Checks whether the given date is valid.
- `now(): T` — Get a new moment
- `parse(value: any, parseFormat: any): T | undefined` — Parse a user-provided value to a Date Object
- `setHours(date: T | undefined, amount: number): T` — Set the hours to the given date.
- `setLocale(locale: any): void` — Sets the locale used for all dates.
- `setMinutes(date: T | undefined, amount: number): T` — Set the minutes to the given date.
- `setSeconds(date: T | undefined, amount: number): T` — Set the seconds to the given date.
- `toIso8601(date: T): string` — Return a Date object as a string, using the ISO standard

### DialogConfig

File: `src/lib/class/dialog-config.class.ts`

Properties:
- `ariaDescribedBy?: string` — ID of the element that describes the dialog.
- `autoFocus: unknown = true` — Whether to focus the dialog when the dialog is isOpen
- `backdropClass?: string | string[] = ''` — Custom class for the backdrop
- `backdropStyle?: any` — Custom style for the backdrop
- `closeOnNavigation?: boolean = true` — Whether the dialog should close when the user goes backwards/forwards in history.
- `data?: any` — Data being injected into the child component
- `disableClose?: unknown = false` — Whether the user can use escape or clicking outside to close a modal
- `event?: PointerEvent` — Pointer Event
- `hasBackdrop?: unknown = true` — Whether the dialog has a backdrop
- `height?: string = ''` — Height of the dialog
- `id?: string` — ID for the modal. If omitted, a unique one will be generated.
- `maxHeight?: number | string` — The max-height of the overlay panel. If a number is provided, pixel units are assumed.
- `maxWidth?: number | string = '85vw'` — The max-width of the overlay panel. If a number is provided, pixel units are assumed.
- `minHeight?: number | string` — The min-height of the overlay panel. If a number is provided, pixel units are assumed.
- `minWidth?: number | string = '18.5em'` — The min-width of the overlay panel. If a number is provided, pixel units are assumed.
- `paneClass?: string | string[] = ''` — Custom class for the pane
- `position?: DialogPosition` — Position overrides
- `role?: "dialog" | "alertdialog" = 'dialog'` — The ARIA role of the dialog element.
- `scrollStrategy?: ScrollStrategy = new NoopScrollStrategy()` — The scroll strategy when the dialog is open Learn more this from https://material.angular.io/cdk/overlay/overview#scroll-strategies
- `viewContainerRef?: ViewContainerRef`
- `width?: string = ''` — Width of the dialog

### DialogRef

File: `src/lib/class/dialog-ref.class.ts`

Properties:
- `componentInstance?: T` — The instance of component isOpen into modal
- `disableClose: unknown = !!this.container.config()?.disableClose` — Whether the user is allowed to close the dialog
- `isAnimating: unknown = this.container.isAnimating`

Methods:
- `afterClosed(): any`
- `afterOpen(): any`
- `backdropClick(): any` — Gets an observable that emits when the overlay's backdrop has been clicked.
- `beforeClose(): any`
- `close(dialogResult?: any): void`
- `keydownEvents(): any` — Gets an observable that emits when keydown events are targeted on the overlay.
- `updatePosition(position?: DialogPosition): unknown` — Updates the dialog's position.
- `updateSize(width: string = 'auto', height: string = 'auto'): unknown` — Updates the dialog's width and height.

### DialogPosition

File: `src/lib/class/dialog-config.class.ts`

Description: Possible overrides for a dialog's position

Properties:
- `bottom?: string` — Override for the dialog's bottom position
- `left?: string` — Override for the dialog's left position
- `right?: string` — Override for the dialog's right position
- `top?: string` — Override for the dialog's top position

### MomentDateTimeAdapterOptions

File: `moment-adapter/src/date-time-adapter.class.ts`

Description: Configurable options for {@see MomentDateAdapter}

Properties:
- `useUtc: boolean` — Turns the use of utc dates on or off. Changing this will change how the DateTimePicker output value. {@default false}

## Public functions

- `provideMomentDateTimeAdapter(): Provider[]`
  Defined in `moment-adapter/src/public-api.ts`
- `provideNativeDateTimeAdapter(): Provider[]`
  Defined in `native-adapter/src/public-api.ts`

## Public type aliases

- `type DateFilter = function`
- `type PickerMode = "popup" | "dialog" | "inline"`
- `type PickerType = "both" | "calendar" | "timer"`
- `type RenderItem = "year" | "month" | "date"`
- `type SelectMode = "single" | "range" | "rangeFrom" | "rangeTo"`
- `type ViewType = "month" | "year" | "multi-years"`

## Public variables

- `NXT_DATE_TIME_FORMATS: unknown = new InjectionToken<DateTimeFormats>('NXT_DATE_TIME_FORMATS')` — InjectionToken for date time picker that can be used to override default format
  Defined in `src/lib/class/date-time-format.class.ts`
- `NXT_MOMENT_DATE_TIME_ADAPTER_OPTIONS: unknown = new InjectionToken<MomentDateTimeAdapterOptions>('NXT_MOMENT_DATE_TIME_ADAPTER_OPTIONS', { providedIn: 'root', factory: NXT_MOMENT_DATE_TIME_ADAPTER_OPTIONS_FAC…` — InjectionToken for moment date adapter to configure options
  Defined in `moment-adapter/src/date-time-adapter.class.ts`
